etymology.description: 心(心) (xīn, "heart") + 囪(囪) as a patterned shape in Han dynasty -> evolved from original cleverness -> later indicating haste
etymology.interpretation: The original form of 悤 (cōng, "clever") depicts a heart 心(心) (xīn, "heart") with a dot indicating a hole, which symbolized intelligence. Over time, the dot evolved into a more complex pattern 囪 during the Han dynasty, transforming the character's meaning to indicate haste, possibly due to the idea of learning quickly or acting with intelligence. This semantic shift is reflected in its derivation to 聰(聰) (cōng, "intelligent") that preserves the original meaning of cleverness.
